title: Docker Buildx CI, Tests, Context, Cache, and Multi-Platform Output · Workflow Trust Lens: Ci Multiplatform Builder Test Evidence
summary: A CI build sends the wrong Git context, ignores a generated file, leaks a credential through build args, trusts a warm cache, skips container behavior tests, and pushes before validation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci multiplatform builder test evidence increment in container build and test workflow. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Build declared platforms with Buildx, distinguish native and emulated execution, test each output, and verify the manifest index and platform digests.
Test image configuration, non-root and read-only behavior, health, signals, resources, network, changed config, and failure before registry mutation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:

  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Buildx workflow leaks a secret through ARG, trusts a fork cache, builds only amd64, pushes before tests, and cannot tie output to source. Separate builder, context, platforms, cache, test, secret, and digest evidence. Competency probe: Build declared platforms with Buildx, distinguish native and emulated execution, test each output, and verify the manifest index and platform digests. A defensible solution applies Ci Multiplatform Builder Test Evidence, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to container build and test workflow.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-multiplatform-builder-test-evidence — Build declared platforms with Buildx, distinguish native and emulated execution, test each output, and verify the manifest index and platform digests.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-ci-docker-build-test-cache-3
  - competency: ci-worked-ci-docker-build-test-cache-3
    artifact: container-build-workflow.yaml
    decision: worked ci docker build test cache 3
    environment: Node 24 TypeScript monorepo with three lockfiles
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      setup-buildx-action: 4
      build-push-action: 7
      context: explicit-path-after-generation
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
      platforms: [linux-amd64, linux-arm64]
      secret: buildkit-secret-mount
      cache-writer: trusted-main-only
      push: after-platform-tests
      build-record: retained
      output-digest: sha256-verified
    verify: render build inputs inspect build record test each platform changed config signals health and read-only behavior then verify index digest
    failure: Reject implicit Git context, ARG secrets, fork-writable cache, build-only proof, one-platform tests, or push-before-validation.
    repair: Select context deliberately, mount secrets, scope cache, test runtime behavior per platform, retain records, and push verified output.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: One amd64 test proves an arm64 image built under emulation works. This fails the Ci Multiplatform Builder Test Evidence contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
